ab: maru cacihli laʉlaʉviku pia Caʉpʉisana, karʉkʉhlʉ kana’ana asapu mahlava cacalaisa.
ch: 我有一個同學叫Caʉpʉna，他常常忘記帶東西。
en: I have a classmate named Caʉpʉna, he often forgets to bring things.

ab: akuku umaiaiapʉ, umahlikua kana’ana laʉlaʉvʉisa umuhli siasuhlatʉ, siasamusu ahla siakatʉka.
ch: 上課的時候，他跟同學借鉛筆、橡皮擦或是筆記本。
en: During class, he borrowed pencils, erasers or notebooks from his classmates.

ab: mungahlʉ, mupapuahlʉ, mutatuluhlu, rianʉ mʉmʉa umauhlisana.
ch: 一次、兩次、三次，大家會借東西給他。
en: Once, twice, three times, everyone would lend him something.

ab: maru a takuliacʉ isa, karʉkʉhlʉ umuhli kua apuaili.
ch: 但是他有個壞習慣，經常借了東西卻不還。
en: But he has a bad habit of often borrowing things but not returning them.

ch: 最後大家都不想借他東西了。
en: In the end, no one wanted to borrow anything from him.

ab: maru ucani ari, kiʉlʉsia amu musa mutatahluvatʉ.
ch: 有一天，我們大家一起去郊遊。
en: One day, we all went on an outing together.

ab: sala aisa, hlatumua Caʉpʉna musa tatiahla, hlaa asapu kana’ana mahlava sipiarisi.
ch: 路途中，Caʉpʉna想上廁所，但他忘記帶衛生紙。
en: On the way, Caʉpʉna wanted to go to the bathroom, but he forgot to bring toilet paper.

ab: umahlikua laʉlaʉvʉisa kana’ana umuhli sipiarisi, ahli’aimau rianʉ mʉmʉa masiaili amihla, kuaku mahlava mʉmʉa.
ch: 他跟同學借衛生紙，可是大家都回他說，我也沒有帶耶。
en: He borrowed toilet paper from his classmates, but everyone told him that I didn’t bring them either.

ab: mutʉngʉtʉngʉ tumangi, umarucarucakʉ kana’ana amihla, “tiaramu mʉmʉa kupauhli ihlaku sipiarisi.”
ch: Caʉpʉna急的哭了出來。他生氣的說：「為什麼大家都不借我衛生紙。」
en: Caʉpʉna burst into tears. He said angrily, why don’t everyone lend me toilet paper!

ab: kihlamuhlamu laʉlaʉvʉisa kana’ana, ta’iaraisaka kuu takacicihli mahlava, hla hliumuhli kuapuaili pahlivinga, ihlau kuu apuaili.
ch: 同學告訴他，因為你都不自己帶，而且借給你的東西，你都不會還!
en: The classmate told him, because you didn’t bring it yourself, and you wouldn’t return the things lent to you!

ab: umaiiapʉ matata, mualualu Caʉpʉna mahlukua ariatʉkʉrana, hliuhliana Caʉpʉna sapialaliacu mʉmʉa.
ch: 隔天上學，Caʉpʉna很早就到學校了，Caʉpʉna把跟同學借的東西都還給同學們。
en: The next day at school, Caʉpʉna arrived at school early and returned everything he had borrowed from his classmates.

ab: umahlikua laʉlaʉvʉisa kana’ana akuainita’ai, hla amihla cucuaari tʉtʉrʉaku takacicihli mahlava pahlivinga, sia’ʉ’ʉraisamana mʉmʉa pauhli ihlaku pahlivinga.
ch: Caʉpʉna向同學們道歉，並說他以後一定會自己帶東西，也謝謝同學們之前願意借他東西。
en: Caʉpʉna apologized to the students and said that he would definitely bring his own things in the future, and thanked the classmates for their willingness to lend him things before.
